conversation.transcript:
Student: Hey, have you seen the new library study rooms?
Librarian: Yes, they just opened last week. They're meant to help with group projects.
Student: That sounds great! Are they easy to reserve?
Librarian: Absolutely, you can book them online through the library website.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the new study rooms?
questions.explain: 新的自習室是為了幫助小組項目而設計的。

questions.q: How can students reserve these study rooms?
options: By signing up at the front desk.
questions.explain: 學生可以通過圖書館網站預約這些自習室。

announcement.transcript: Attention students! The campus bookstore will be holding a sale this weekend on textbooks and supplies. Everything will be 20% off. Don't miss this opportunity to save on your essential items. We hope to see you there!

questions.q: What will be on sale this weekend?
questions.explain: 本週末將有課本和用品的特賣。

questions.q: How much discount can students get?

talk.transcript: Today, we will discuss the concept of cognitive dissonance. Cognitive dissonance occurs when an individual holds two or more contradictory beliefs, values, or ideas simultaneously. This mental conflict can lead to discomfort, motivating the person to change their beliefs or behaviors to restore harmony. For example, a student who values health but smokes may experience dissonance. They could either quit smoking to align with their health values or rationalize their behavior by believing it's not that harmful. Understanding cognitive dissonance is crucial in psychology as it explains how people strive for internal consistency.

options: A mental conflict from contradictory beliefs.
A method of learning new information.
questions.explain: 認知不協調是由矛盾信念引起的心理衝突。

questions.q: What motivates a person experiencing cognitive dissonance?
options: To change their beliefs or behaviors.
questions.explain: 經歷認知不協調的人會被動力改變其信念或行為。

questions.q: What example is given related to cognitive dissonance?
options: A person learning a new skill.
A group working on a project.
questions.explain: 舉例中提到了一位重視健康的吸煙者。

questions.q: Why is understanding cognitive dissonance important?
options: It explains how people strive for consistency.
It is useful for learning languages.
questions.explain: 理解認知不協調有助於解釋人們如何努力保持一致性。
